HORIZONTAL_PUSH.description: Use the largest pain-free range of motion possible. Full ROM improves muscle recruitment and hypertrophy.

VERTICAL_PUSH.description: Lower the weight under control and press through the complete overhead range without compromising shoulder position.

HORIZONTAL_PULL.description: Allow the shoulder blades to protract during the eccentric and fully retract during the concentric.

VERTICAL_PULL.description: Reach a full stretch overhead before pulling through the entire range.

SQUAT.description: Use the deepest pain-free squat depth that maintains proper technique and spinal neutrality.

HINGE.description: Maintain a full hip hinge while preserving a neutral spine and hamstring tension.

LUNGE.description: Lower until both knees achieve appropriate depth while maintaining balance.

ELBOW_FLEXION.description: Fully extend the elbow without losing tension and curl through the complete range.

ELBOW_EXTENSION.description: Allow full elbow flexion before extending without using momentum.

SHOULDER_ABDUCTION.description: Raise the arms through a comfortable range while avoiding excessive shoulder elevation.

CALF.description: Pause at both the fully stretched and fully contracted positions for maximum stimulus.

CORE_FLEXION.description: Move through controlled spinal flexion while avoiding momentum.

ANTI_EXTENSION.description: Static exercise—focus on maintaining posture rather than movement range.

ROTATION.description: Rotate under control through a comfortable range while maintaining core stability.

CARRY.description: Loaded carries are distance/time-based rather than ROM-dependent.
